comments.author: example-reader
comments.text: 'Refactoring on reflex' names the specific bad move. It feels responsible — you're 'cleaning up' — but without investigation it's just pattern-matching on surface features.

comments.author: example-reader
comments.text: 'The work is the investigation, not the conclusion.' Whole essay collapses to this line. Worth memorizing.

comments.author: example-reader
comments.text: The double-failure framing is the underappreciated part. It's not just 'we might refactor good code away' — it's simultaneously 'we might preserve bad code because nobody looked.'
